title: Agentic AI startup Quash raised $635K Pre-seed funding from Arali Ventures – Incubees
full_text:
Agentic AI startup Quash raised $635K Pre-seed funding from Arali Ventures
Quash, an AI startup based in Bengaluru, has raised $635,000 in pre-seed funding led by Arali Ventures. Java Capital, PeerCheque, DeVC by Matrix Partners, and Abhishek Goyal, a co-founder of Tracxn, h
Quash, an AI startup based in Bengaluru, has raised $635,000 in pre-seed funding led by Arali Ventures. Java Capital, PeerCheque, DeVC by Matrix Partners, and Abhishek Goyal, a co-founder of Tracxn, have also contributed.
By using autonomous QA agents that automate the whole testing process—from finding bugs to fixing them—Quash is transforming the testing of mobile apps and doing away with the need for human testers.
By improving their AI QA platform, growing their engineering team, and extending their community initiatives, this investment will hasten Quash's development and implementation of AI-driven QA solutions.
Quash has introduced Appstronauts, a community for mobile developers, and Quash Report, an open-source bug reporting tool that is currently being used by hundreds of developers and more than 11 companies.
Quash, under the direction of Ayush Shrivastava, seeks to update QA procedures so that engineering teams can concentrate on creativity. Quash uses AI to improve software releases' speed and accuracy while resolving inefficiencies in conventional QA processes.

By using autonomous QA agents that automate the whole testing process—from finding bugs to fixing them—Quash is transforming the testing of mobile apps and doing away with the need for human testers.
By improving their AI QA platform, growing their engineering team, and extending their community initiatives, this investment will hasten Quash's development and implementation of AI-driven QA solutions.
